key_strengths: 50–63% global market share in semiconductor process control — a near-monopoly position
Switching costs among the highest in semiconductor equipment; mid-node replacement is prohibitively costly
60%+ gross margins sustained through a full semiconductor cycle including the 2023 downturn
ROIC of 37–42% through full cycles; $3.7B+ annual FCF on only ~3% capex intensity
17 consecutive annual dividend increases at ~16% CAGR; $11B total buyback capacity as of March 2026
AI-enhanced defect classification flywheel compounds competitive advantage with installed base growth
Stress-test survivor: FCF remains positive and dividend sustainable even in a sustained 30% revenue decline
key_risks: WFE cycle downturn: semiconductor capex cycles are normal; prior corrections caused 40–55% stock drawdowns
China export controls: China was ~30% of FY2025 revenue ($3.6B); further U.S.-China tech decoupling is structural
Multiple compression: trailing P/E of ~43x is 93% above 10-year average; mean reversion alone implies 30–35% downside
Taiwan concentration: ~25–27% of revenue from Taiwan (TSMC); Taiwan Strait tail risk is extreme if realized
Chinese domestic competitors: state-backed NAURA and SMEE are funded to develop process control alternatives
red_flags: All three DCF scenarios (bear $500, base $850, bull $1,280) imply downside from current ~$1,465 price
Risk asymmetry is ~6:1 unfavorable: bear-case loss (~63%) vastly exceeds bull-case gain (~nil)
360% stock appreciation from end-2022 to early 2026 driven partly by P/E multiple expansion, not just earnings growth
Taiwan + China combined represent ~55–57% of revenue — both geographies carry elevated geopolitical risk
CEO insider ownership of only 0.017%; no publicly visible succession planning for 20-year CEO
buy_triggers: Price declines to ~$850–900 (base case DCF intrinsic value, ~40% below current price)
Price declines to ~$650–700 (genuine margin of safety entry, ~55% below current price)
Severe WFE cycle downturn causes panic selling below intrinsic value while moat remains structurally intact
sell_triggers: KLA loses a design-in at any leading-edge fab node (TSMC, Samsung, SK Hynix) to any competitor
China export controls expand to cover all KLA product categories, permanently removing >$2B annual revenue
EPS grows into valuation but stock still trades at 35x+ forward earnings in FY2028 or beyond — trim on multiple failure to compress
valuation_summary: At ~$1,465/share, KLAC trades at ~43x trailing P/E and ~34x forward P/E — roughly double its 10-year historical average of ~22x; all three DCF scenarios (bear $500, base $850, bull $1,280) imply downside from the current price, with the probability-weighted intrinsic value approximately ~$860, suggesting ~41% downside; a genuine margin-of-safety entry requires a price of approximately $650–700.
source_summary: Analysis based on web search results from March 2026, KLA Corporation IR press releases (FY2025 Q4 earnings, March 2026 Investor Day), KLA Q2 FY2026 10-Q, MacroTrends, GuruFocus, StockAnalysis, and PortfoliosLab; all financial data is current as of March 12, 2026 with FY2025 figures for the fiscal year ended June 30, 2025.
